payload.title: task create --from-file loses acceptance criteria in lifecycle authority
payload.summary: While creating User Site tasks 11 and 12 from markdown files, the generated task markdown contained Acceptance Criteria, but narada task read showed acceptance_criteria as empty until a separate narada task amend --criteria command was run. This creates extra operator friction and risks divergence between visible task markdown and lifecycle SQLite authority.
evidence: Task 12 markdown initially contained four acceptance criteria under ## Acceptance Criteria.
Immediately after task create --from-file, narada task read 12 returned acceptance_criteria: [] and unchecked_count: 0.
Running narada task amend 12 --criteria populated lifecycle authority correctly.
proposal: Make task create --from-file parse Acceptance Criteria into lifecycle authority, or reject/diagnose from-file bodies whose criteria cannot be admitted.
Add regression coverage for from-file task creation preserving criteria in both markdown and lifecycle SQLite projection.
payload.recommendation: Create an execution task in Narada proper for task create --from-file criteria admission.

task.title: Preserve from-file task criteria in lifecycle authority
